must-have.name: Move Contracts — Round Management + Escrow + Settlement
must-have.description: shadowbook.move with Coin<DUSDC> escrow at commit, commit-reveal state machine, parimutuel settlement (resolve_round + claim_payout), cancel_round. All critic fixes applied: address::to_bytes for hash, remove commitment after reveal, round_id in SEAL identity.

must-have.name: SEAL Time-Lock Encryption Integration
must-have.description: seal_policy.move (time-lock with round_id + timestamp), frontend SealClient encrypt/decrypt flow, Walrus blob storage for encrypted orders, session key management. LocalStorage backup for order data.

must-have.name: Trader Frontend — Full Commit-Reveal UI
must-have.description: Next.js 15 app with dApp-kit-react v2 wallet connection. Pages: home (active rounds), round detail (commit form → reveal button → results). Full flow: connect wallet → commit encrypted order → wait → reveal → claim payout. Dark/distinctive design (no AI slop).

should-have.name: DeepBook Predict Direct Integration
should-have.description: deepbook_integration.move wrapper — after reveal, mint binary positions via PTB on DeepBook Predict testnet. Proves real DeepBook usage for the $70K track.

should-have.name: Admin Panel — Round Creation UI
should-have.description: Simple admin page to create rounds (oracle, strike, durations). Saves time during demo vs CLI.

should-have.name: Historical Rounds Display
should-have.description: Show past rounds with results, payouts, and participant count.

could-have.name: Multi-Oracle Support
could-have.description: Support multiple price oracles (BTC, ETH, SOL) per round.

could-have.name: Leaderboard & Stats
could-have.description: Trader leaderboard, win/loss tracking, volume stats.

wont-have.name: Auto-reveal Bot
wont-have.description: Automated service that watches for time-lock expiry and auto-reveals. Too complex for solo hackathon.
